content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 21, 1988

W.H. Luking
City Hall, Room 601
121 N. LaSalle
Chicago, IL 60602

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Advice File No. I-88-125

Dear Mr. Luking:

On March 25, 1988, we received your letter requesting a
copy of our organic statute and our rules and regulations.

The Political Reform Act 1/ was an initiative on the 1974
ballot. It was passed overwhelmingly by the voters. The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on
public officials. A copy of the Act is enclosed.

The regulations which implement the Political Reform Act
can be ordered through the Department of General Services,
Documents Section, P.O. Box 1015, North Highlands, CA 95660.
To order the regulations, you cite Title 2, Division 6 of the
California Code of Regulations. There is an $8 base fee and a
$12 subscription fee to keep the regulations current.

If we can be of further assistance, please feel free to
call us at (916) 322-5901.

Diane Griffiths
General Counsel

by: Jeanette Turvill
Legal Assistant
Legal Division

DMG:JET:da

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Dear Sir or Madame:

MAR 25 12:10 PM '86

I would like to obtain any information that may be available on your Agency, including a copy of your Organic Statute and any other regulations.

Thank you.

W.H. Luking
Room 601
City Hall
121 N. LaSalle
Chicago, Ill.
60602

[RECEIVED stamp showing: MAR 22 1989, Secretary of State]

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 28, 1988


W. H. Luking
City Hall
121 N. LaSalle, Room 601
Chicago, IL  60602

Re:  87-125

Dear Mr. Luking:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on March 25, 1988 by the Fair Political Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for a copy of the FPPC's organic statute and rules and regulations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided a copy of the Political Reform Act and directed the requester to order the implementing regulations (Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations) from the Department of General Services with information about fees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate correspondence pieces. No formal Q/A or conclusion sections present; this is administrative correspondence responding to a records request. The 'question' involves requesting copies of statutes and regulations rather than requesting legal advice on compliance matters.

QUESTION: The requester asked for a copy of the FPPC's organic statute and rules and regulations.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provided a copy of the Political Reform Act and directed the requester to order the implementing regulations (Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations) from the Department of General Services with information about fees.

embedding.summary: This document consists of two letters from the FPPC responding to W.H. Luking's March 25, 1988 request for copies of the Political Reform Act and FPPC regulations. The first letter acknowledges receipt and outlines the expected timeline for responses, while the second provides the requested statutory and regulatory information with ordering instructions.
